_comment: Single source of truth for numeric protocol/safety constants that must agree across the ESP32 firmware (C) and/or the dashboard (JS). Each group's "_targets" selects which generated files get it — most are firmware-internal (c); wire-framing values the dashboard also encodes carry js too. Edit this file, then run `make gen-constants` (also runs automatically on `make compile`). Do NOT edit the generated files directly — `tools/gen-constants.py` will overwrite them.

chunked_transfer._comment: Shared begin/chunk/commit opcode framing used by OTA. OTA_OP_ABORT is OTA-specific but lives here alongside the framing it extends.

fs_transfer._comment: On-robot file service framing (main/fs_svc.c <-> docs/fs/fs-client.js). Two characteristics, each with a 1-byte leading opcode. FS_OP (browser->board write): FS_OP_JSON carries a JSON control request {op,...}; FS_OP_WBYTES carries raw file bytes for the open write session (like OTA_DATA chunks). FS_DATA (board->browser notify): a JSON response stream (BEGIN[u32 len]/CHUNK/END, mirroring OPS_RESPONSE) and a binary file-read stream (FILE_BEGIN[u32 len]/FILE_CHUNK/FILE_END, mirroring SNAPSHOT_DATA). The two FS_DATA streams never interleave — a read reply sends its JSON header first, then the file stream.

script_stream._comment: On-robot Python VM output stream (main/pyvm.c -> the IDE). Each SCRIPT_OUTPUT notify leads with a 1-byte opcode: TEXT carries UTF-8 stdout as the script runs; DONE ends a clean run; ERROR carries the traceback text of an uncaught exception. Run/stop are ops verbs (script-run{name} / script-stop) on the OPS char, not a new characteristic.

hub_broker._comment: MQTT-over-WebSockets listener port — fixed convention with the hub's pi/mosquitto.example.conf (sprocket-robotics/hub CONTRACT.md). js-only: in this repo only the dashboard (hub transport + pair signaling/lobby) dials the broker; robot firmware never does.
